prompt: Quella sera ____ a lungo con mia madre. (parlare, io)
explanation: When you tell of a single completed event finished in the past, the simple past you reach for in writing is the passato remoto, the remote past. A regular -are verb takes -ai in the io form: parlare gives parlai. 'Quella sera' fixes a finished past, so the present parlo and the imperfect parlavo (a habit or ongoing state) are both wrong here.

prompt: Quella sera Marco ____ a lungo davanti a tutti. (parlare)
explanation: The lui/lei form of a regular -are passato remoto ends in a stressed -ò, and the written accent is obligatory: parlare gives parlò. Drop the accent and you have written the present parlo instead, a different tense.

prompt: Quell'estate noi ____ benissimo ogni sera. (mangiare)
explanation: The noi form of the passato remoto doubles the m: -ammo. mangiare gives mangiammo. The single-m mangiamo is the present, and mangiavamo is the imperfect; 'quell'estate ... benissimo' frames a single remembered occasion, so the remote past is wanted.

prompt: I soldati ____ all'alba e occuparono la città. (arrivare)
explanation: The loro form of a regular -are passato remoto ends in -arono: arrivare gives arrivarono. The clipped arrivarno is a frequent spoken slip; the present arrivano is the wrong tense. The companion verb occuparono shows the same pattern, anchoring the past.

prompt: Non gli ____ affatto, quella volta. (credere, io)
explanation: Regular -ere verbs accept two standard io endings in the passato remoto: -ei or -etti. credere gives credei or credetti, and neither is preferred over the other. Watch the spelling: the -ire double-i form credii is wrong, and credetti has a single d.
examiner_note: Dual -ei/-etti: both accepted at full credit per the leaf description. Diagnostic miss is borrowing the -ire pattern (credii).

prompt: Nessuno ci ____, all'inizio. (credere)
explanation: The lui/lei form of a regular -ere passato remoto is also dual: credé (with an obligatory acute accent) or credette. The bare crede is the present, and the -ire accent credì belongs to verbs like dormì, not to credere.

prompt: Quell'anno ____ la vecchia casa di famiglia. (vendere, io)
explanation: Another regular -ere verb with the dual io ending: vendere gives vendei or vendetti. Both are fully standard. 'Quell'anno' marks a single completed sale, the remote past.

prompt: I soldati non ____ il pericolo e avanzarono. (temere)
explanation: The loro form is dual as well: temerono or temettero. The clipped temerno and the present-shaped temettono are wrong, and temevano is the imperfect. The companion avanzarono pins the remote past.

prompt: Quella notte non ____ affatto. (dormire, io)
explanation: A regular -ire verb takes -ii in the io passato remoto, giving a written double i: dormire gives dormii. The single-i dormi is the present (or an imperative), the accented dormì is the lui/lei form, and dormivo is the imperfect. 'Quella notte ... non affatto' frames one finished night.

prompt: Il bambino ____ profondamente fino al mattino. (dormire)
explanation: The lui/lei form of an -ire passato remoto ends in a stressed -ì with an obligatory written accent: dormire gives dormì. Drop the accent and you have the present dormi; dormiva is the imperfect.

prompt: Solo allora noi ____ la verità. (capire)
explanation: The passato remoto has no -isc- infix: capire builds straight on the stem, so the noi form is capimmo, never capiscemmo. The doubled m marks the remote past against the present capiamo; capivamo is the imperfect.
examiner_note: Left visible (not suppressed): a 'Regular -ire' breadcrumb tempts the catchable -isc- over-extension capiscemmo, which must_not_include catches. Productive-trap case per criterion 15.

prompt: Quella mattina ____ il primo treno per Roma. (prendere, io)
explanation: A large group of -ere verbs has an irregular 'strong' stem that shows up in just three persons (io, lui/lei, loro), with the io ending -i landing straight on it: prendere gives presi. Treat it as regular (prendei, prendetti) and you have missed the strong stem that is the whole point.
examiner_note: Strong-class membership is not derivable from the -ere infinitive, so suppressed per criterion 15.

prompt: Quella mattina tu ____ il primo treno per Roma. (prendere)
explanation: The strong stem appears in only three persons. The tu form is perfectly regular and built on the ordinary stem: prendere gives prendesti, not presesti. Carrying the strong stem into the middle persons (presesti) is the signature error.
examiner_note: Left visible: a 'strong 1-3-3' breadcrumb tempts presesti, the catchable over-extension caught by must_not_include. Half the diagnostic of this leaf is the regular middle person.

prompt: I ragazzi ____ l'ultimo autobus della sera. (prendere)
explanation: The loro form sits on the strong stem with the ending -ero: prendere gives presero. The regularised prenderono / prendettero ignore the strong stem.

prompt: Quella notte ____ una lunga lettera. (scrivere, io)
explanation: scrivere has the strong stem scriss-: the io form is scrissi, with a double s. The regularised scrivei/scrivetti miss the strong stem, and the single-s scrisi mis-spells it.

prompt: Manzoni ____ il suo romanzo nell'Ottocento. (scrivere)
explanation: The lui/lei form is scrisse, strong stem scriss- plus -e. Manzoni and the historical frame pin the remote past, the tense of literary and biographical narrative.

prompt: Quel giorno la ____ per la prima volta. (vedere, io)
explanation: vedere has the strong stem vid-: the io form is vidi. The regularised vedei/vedetti and the doubled viddi are all wrong.

prompt: Tutti ____ la cometa quella notte. (vedere)
explanation: The loro form is videro, strong stem vid- plus -ero. Note the single d against the tempting viddero.

prompt: Lei ____ il libro sul tavolo e uscì. (mettere)
explanation: mettere has the strong stem mis-: the lui/lei form is mise, with a single s. The regularised mettè/mettette and the double-s messe (the participle messo's relative) are wrong.

prompt: Quella sera ____ tutto in ordine prima di partire. (mettere, noi)
explanation: The noi form is regular, on the ordinary stem: mettere gives mettemmo, not misemmo. The strong stem mis- stays out of the middle persons.
examiner_note: Left visible: the breadcrumb tempts the catchable misemmo (strong stem in a middle person).

prompt: Allora le ____ scusa per tutto. (chiedere, io)
explanation: chiedere has the strong stem chies-: the io form is chiesi. Do not confuse it with the past participle chiesto or write chiesti.

prompt: Il professore ____ la poesia ad alta voce. (leggere)
explanation: leggere has the strong stem less-: the lui/lei form is lesse, with a double s and a hard-to-soft change from gg to ss. The regularised leggé/leggette miss the strong stem.

prompt: I miei nonni ____ in campagna per molti anni. (vivere)
explanation: vivere has the strong stem viss-: the loro form is vissero. The regularised viverono/vivettero and the mis-formed vissono are all wrong.

prompt: Quel pomeriggio ____ a trovarci all'improvviso. (venire)
explanation: venire is an -ire verb that follows the strong pattern: the strong stem venn- gives the lui/lei form venne, with a double n. The accented venì would be the regular -ire shape, which venire does not take; veniva is the imperfect.

prompt: Voi quando ____ a Roma per la prima volta? (venire)
explanation: Even venire keeps the middle persons regular: the voi form is veniste, on the ordinary stem, not venneste. The strong stem venn- stays in the io/lui/loro forms only.
examiner_note: Left visible: tempts the catchable venneste. Voi is supplied, so the cue names the verb only (criterion 13 cue economy).

prompt: Quell'anno ____ davvero felice. (essere, io)
explanation: essere is wholly suppletive in the passato remoto, built on an unrelated stem: fui, fosti, fu, fummo, foste, furono. The io form is fui. There is no regular essei.

prompt: Il viaggio ____ lungo e difficile. (essere)
explanation: The lui/lei form is fu, with no accent. fu is one of the commonest words in Italian narrative. The imperfect era describes an ongoing state; fu states the event as a whole.

prompt: Quelli ____ anni molto difficili. (essere)
explanation: The loro form is furono. The clipped furno is a common spoken reduction; the imperfect erano frames a background state rather than the bounded stretch the remote past gives.

prompt: In quel momento ____ una gran paura. (avere, io)
explanation: avere follows the strong 1-3-3 pattern on the stem ebb-: ebbi, avesti, ebbe, avemmo, aveste, ebbero. The io form is ebbi, with a double b. The regularised avei/avetti and the single-b ebi are wrong. ebbi is also one auxiliary of the trapassato remoto.

prompt: Allora ____ un'idea improvvisa. (avere)
explanation: The lui/lei form is ebbe, on the strong stem ebb-. ebbe is extremely frequent in narrative ('ebbe paura', 'ebbe un figlio'). The regularised avé/avette miss the strong stem.

prompt: Tu ____ ragione fin dall'inizio. (avere)
explanation: avere keeps its middle persons regular, on the stem av-: the tu form is avesti, not ebbesti. The strong stem ebb- appears in the io/lui/loro forms only; avevi is the imperfect.
examiner_note: Left visible: tempts the catchable ebbesti (strong stem in a middle person).

prompt: Quel giorno ____ una scelta difficile. (fare, io)
explanation: fare has the strong stem fec-: feci, facesti, fece, facemmo, faceste, fecero. The io form is feci. The regularised facei/facetti and the clipped fei are wrong.

prompt: Leonardo ____ molti disegni e studi. (fare)
explanation: The lui/lei form is fece, on the strong stem fec-. It is very common in biographical narrative. The middle persons keep the regular stem fac- (facesti, facemmo, faceste).

prompt: Mi ____ la mano e se ne andò. (dare)
explanation: dare has two accepted forms in the strong persons: diede or dette in the lui/lei (diedi/detti in the io). Both are standard. Do not confuse with the present dà or the imperfect dava.
examiner_note: Dual diede/dette flagged for the project author in the coverage report; both accepted at full credit.

prompt: Quella sera ____ a casa da solo. (stare, io)
explanation: stare has the strong stem stett-: stetti, stesti, stette, stemmo, steste, stettero. The io form is stetti, with a double t. The regularised stei and the imperfect stavo are wrong.

prompt: Non ____ una sola parola in tutta la sera. (dire)
explanation: dire has the strong stem diss-: dissi, dicesti, disse, dicemmo, diceste, dissero. The lui/lei form is disse, with a double s. The middle persons keep the regular stem dic- (dicesti, dicemmo).

prompt: Appena ____ di parlare, tutti applaudirono. (finire)
explanation: The trapassato remoto sets one completed action just before another in a narrative. It pairs the passato remoto of the auxiliary with the past participle: here the avere form ebbe plus finito gives 'ebbe finito'. 'Appena ... applaudirono' (itself a remote past) is the exact frame this rare, literary tense lives in. Use the pluperfect aveva finito and you have the everyday past, not the remote one.
examiner_note: Two markpoints per criterion 8: the passato remoto auxiliary (this tree) and the participle (PP tree). The pluperfect aveva is the diagnostic miss; the simple passato remoto finì misses the auxiliary markpoint.

prompt: Dopo che ____ partito, ci pentimmo della lite. (partire)
explanation: partire is a verb of motion, so its trapassato remoto takes essere, not avere: the passato remoto essere form fu plus the participle partito gives 'fu partito'. The participle agrees with the masculine singular subject. 'Dopo che ... pentimmo' frames the prior completed departure. The pluperfect era partito would be the everyday past.
examiner_note: Two markpoints: essere passato remoto auxiliary (this tree) and the participle, masculine-singular agreement. A feminine subject would need partita.

prompt: Quando ____ le valigie, uscirono di casa. (fare)
explanation: The loro auxiliary in the passato remoto is ebbero, and fare's irregular participle is fatto, giving the trapassato remoto 'ebbero fatto'. 'Quando ... uscirono' sets it just before the main remote-past event. The pluperfect avevano fatto is the ordinary past, not this literary one.
examiner_note: Two markpoints: avere passato remoto auxiliary 3pl (this tree) and fare's irregular participle (PP irregular tree).
